glossary.definition: Active Scan is a ZAP scanning concept used to discover or test target behavior.
Active Scan Rules is a ZAP scanning concept used to discover or test target behavior.
Add-on is a core ZAP concept that supports the overall testing workflow.
Alert relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
Alert Evidence relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
Alert Filters relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
Alerts Tab is a desktop UI element used to inspect or control scans.
API is part of the ZAP API used to automate or retrieve results.
API Key is part of the ZAP API used to automate or retrieve results.
Attack Mode is a ZAP mode that changes which actions are allowed.
Automation Framework is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
Automation Plan is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
AJAX Spider is a ZAP scanning concept used to discover or test target behavior.
Automation Environment is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
Automation GUI is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
Automation Jobs is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
Automation Vars is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.
Attack Surface is a security testing concept used with ZAP.
Authentication Method relates to authentication or session handling in ZAP.
Authentication Verification relates to authentication or session handling in ZAP.
Alerts Count relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
Alert Risk relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
Alert Confidence relates to how ZAP records, presents, or prioritizes findings.
API UI is part of the ZAP API used to automate or retrieve results.
Automation -autorun is part of the Automation Framework for repeatable scans.

cheatsheets.title: Quick Start Checklist
items: Confirm you have permission to test the target.
Enter a full URL including http/https.
Choose traditional or AJAX spider based on the app.
Review alerts immediately after the scan completes.

cheatsheets.title: Modes At-a-Glance
items: Protected: dangerous actions only in scope.
Attack: active scan new nodes in scope automatically.

cheatsheets.title: Context & Scope Setup
items: Set the context in scope before scanning.

cheatsheets.title: User & Auth Setup
items: Define authentication method in the context.
Run scans as the desired user.

cheatsheets.title: Automation Framework
items: Check exit codes: 0 success, 1 error, 2 warnings.
Define contexts and URLs in env section.

cheatsheets.title: Docker Full Scan
items: Run zap-full-scan.py with a target URL.
Mount a work dir for reports.
Use exit codes to control CI.

cheatsheets.title: ZAPit Recon
items: Include -cmd or it will be ignored.
Review tech detection and alert summary.
Use findings to plan deeper scans.

cheatsheets.title: Spider Choice
items: Traditional spider is fast for HTML links.

cheatsheets.title: Scan Policy Basics
items: Tune strength and thresholds per target.

cheatsheets.title: API Access
items: API UI available at http://zap/ when proxying.
Configure API access in Options if needed.
Restrict API access to trusted hosts.
Use API for automation and reporting.

cheatsheets.title: UI Navigation
items: Alerts tab lists findings by risk.

cheatsheets.title: Manual Explore
items: Launch browser from Quick Start Manual Explore.

cheatsheets.title: Automation Environment
items: Define contexts with names and URLs.
Avoid default ports in URLs when required.
Use vars and configs for repeatability.

cheatsheets.title: Exit Codes
items: 0: Plan completed with no errors or warnings.
Override with exitStatus job if needed.

cheatsheets.title: Packaged Scans
items: Full scan: spider + optional AJAX + active scan.
Use reports and exit codes for CI.

cheatsheets.title: Scan Safety
items: Only scan targets you have permission to test.
Use Protected mode and defined scope.

cheatsheets.title: CI/CD Integration
items: Fail builds on errors or high-risk alerts.

cheatsheets.title: Troubleshooting
items: Review automation logs and exit codes.

cheatsheets.title: Automation Configs
items: Use configs section to set ZAP options.

cheatsheets.title: API UI Reminders
items: API UI available at http://zap/ when proxying.
Restrict API access to trusted hosts.
Use API key for sensitive actions.
Disable risky API options in shared environments.
